You can easily manage all project dependencies using the requirements.txt file.

items.name: Credit-Card-Fraud-Detection-using-Sparse-Autoencoder-and-XGBoost
items.description: A machine learning framework for credit card fraud detection using Sparse Autoencoder-based feature extraction and XGBoost classification. The project addresses the challenge of highly imbalanced financial transaction data through preprocessing, feature learning, and model optimization.

items.name: sm2rain_hydroai
items.description: sm2rain_hydroai is an AI-enhanced SM2RAIN framework for estimating rainfall from satellite soil moisture. It combines physical inversion with machine learning and deep learning to improve rainfall reconstruction, extreme event monitoring, and hydrologic analysis across climates and regions.

The best-performing model was identified through error metric analysis and subsequently hybridized with machine learning models to capture both linear and non-linear patterns in financial data. Designed a dashboard for the results

It employs a Hybrid Ensemble Feature Selection (HEFS) methodology and compares tuned models such as Random Forest, XGBoost, CNN, and TCN via meta-learning layers to provide a robust solution for identifying malicious URLs.

items.name: Code-of-Optimization-Enhanced-Machine-Failure-Classification-Using-Critical-Sensor-Features-study

Three models, including Light Gradient Boosting (LGBC), Random Forest Classification (RFC), XGBoost Classification (XGBC), and a hybrid voting ensemble, were developed and subsequently optimized using the Tasmanian Devil Optimization (TDO) algorithm.
